seoTitle: Mackenzie River vs Macknade — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mackenzie River and Macknade. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mackenzie River vs Macknade: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mackenzie River and Macknade.
heading: Mackenzie River vs Macknade — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mackenzie River, Queensland and Macknade, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mackenzie River has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Macknade (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mackenzie River or Macknade safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mackenzie River has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Macknade scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mackenzie River scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Macknade scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mackenzie River or Macknade?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mackenzie River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
